title: The Missed Medication
description: Hypertensive patient BP spikes. Anamnesis reveals stopped ACE inhibitor due to cough.

triggering_alert.alert_message: Critical BP 178/108 mmHg, 6 consecutive high readings.

key_ehr_facts: Last visit: BP well-controlled at 128/82 two months ago
key_anamnesis_facts: Patient stopped Lisinopril 10 days ago due to persistent dry cough

gold_standard_brief.primary_concern: Hypertensive urgency secondary to self-discontinuation of ACE inhibitor due to cough side effect.

title: The False Alarm
description: Diabetic patient glucose alert. Anamnesis reveals planned dietary change - alert is benign.
key_ehr_facts: Physician note: expect transient glucose fluctuation during titration
key_anamnesis_facts: Patient attended celebration dinner last night
Fasting glucose this morning was 142 mg/dL

gold_standard_brief.primary_concern: Post-prandial glucose spike attributable to dietary deviation during medication titration. Likely benign.

title: The Silent Deterioration
description: Heart failure patient gradual weight gain over 2 weeks. Trend suggests fluid retention.

triggering_alert.alert_message: Gradual weight gain 6.4 kg over 14 days.

key_anamnesis_facts: Shortness of breath walking to bathroom

gold_standard_brief.primary_concern: High suspicion acute decompensated heart failure with fluid retention.

title: The Incomplete Record
description: Transferred patient. EHR sparse. System must rely on anamnesis and flag gaps.

triggering_alert.alert_message: Critical BP 168/102 mmHg in recently registered patient.

key_ehr_facts: EHR imported from external system - HIGH INCOMPLETENESS
Only available: Name, DOB, one medication (Ramipril 5mg)
No lab results, no visit notes
key_anamnesis_facts: Also reports anxiety disorder on sertraline
Reports Ramipril causes dizziness - considering stopping

gold_standard_brief.primary_concern: BP elevation in newly transferred patient with critically incomplete EHR.
recommended_actions: Obtain complete medication list from patient

title: The Conflicting Data
description: Patient reports full adherence but lab results show sub-therapeutic drug levels.

triggering_alert.alert_message: SpO2 93% in atrial fibrillation patient on anticoagulation.

key_anamnesis_facts: Patient insists she takes ALL medications exactly as prescribed
Reports new fatigue and mild shortness of breath for 1 week

gold_standard_brief.primary_concern: Clinically significant discrepancy between reported adherence and sub-therapeutic drug levels. Elevated stroke risk.
